  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",

    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should we treat the reported FP/FN metrics as authoritative (and revise claims to match them), or treat the stated superiority claim as authoritative (and infer the numbers are placeholders/errors to be corrected)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening likely requires reframing the core contribution/claim to match what the results can support.",
    "R1.2": "The current argument contradicts its own reported numbers, so ensuring internal logical consistency is load-bearing.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about error reduction must align with the FP/FN rates presented, making evidence-claim linkage critical.",
    "R1.6": "Concise, non-redundant wording and stronger phrasing are needed to make the passage more forceful.",
    "RX.1": "Explicitly acknowledging uncertainty or conflicts between claims and metrics is necessary for credible strengthening.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding unsupported improvement claims is essential to prevent overstatement of reliability.",
    "RX.3": "The work should focus on strengthening this passage without drifting into unrelated sections or experiments.",
    "RX.4": "Given the clinical context, preventing overclaims reduces the risk of downstream misuse or harm.",
    "RX.5": "Any strengthened claims should be traceable to the specific reported numbers and benchmark descriptions."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should we treat the reported FP/FN metrics as authoritative (and revise claims to match them), or treat the stated superiority claim as authoritative (and infer the numbers are placeholders/errors to be corrected)?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening likely requires reframing the core contribution/claim to match what the results can support.
rationale.R1.2: The current argument contradicts its own reported numbers, so ensuring internal logical consistency is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about error reduction must align with the FP/FN rates presented, making evidence-claim linkage critical.
rationale.R1.6: Concise, non-redundant wording and stronger phrasing are needed to make the passage more forceful.
rationale.RX.1: Explicitly acknowledging uncertainty or conflicts between claims and metrics is necessary for credible strengthening.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding unsupported improvement claims is essential to prevent overstatement of reliability.
rationale.RX.3: The work should focus on strengthening this passage without drifting into unrelated sections or experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Given the clinical context, preventing overclaims reduces the risk of downstream misuse or harm.
rationale.RX.5: Any strengthened claims should be traceable to the specific reported numbers and benchmark descriptions.
